name: Gen Z AI Tools - Carousel Builder (Workflow C)

nodes.name: Google Sheets Trigger - Row Updated
nodes.notes: Carousels share the SAME 'Reels' tab as the tweet-card reels (user's choice 2026-06-19). The IF node below separates them by 'Post Type' == 'carousel'. If you ever move carousels to their own tab, change this sheetName AND set the GOOGLE_SHEET_CAROUSELS_NAME env var on the GitHub side to match, so the cloud draft step reads the same tab.

nodes.name: IF - Carousel Ready to Run
nodes.notes: Fires only when a row's Status == 'Ready to Run' (trimmed, case-insensitive) AND 'Post Type' == 'carousel' AND it has a Topic. The Post Type gate is what splits carousels from reels on the shared tab. 'Ready to Run' is the single GO signal; it is deliberately different from the terminal 'Ready to Post' so this every-minute trigger can never re-fire a finished carousel into a loop.

nodes.name: Set - Extract Row Fields
nodes.notes: Carry the sheet row number + Topic for the claim and the failure-email. The cloud build re-reads everything it needs from the sheet itself (it finds the next 'Ready to Run' carousel row by Status + Post Type), so n8n does not need to pass any content.

nodes.name: Sheets - Claim (Drafting)
nodes.notes: Loop guard: flip Status off 'Ready to Run' to 'Drafting' the moment we start, so the every-minute trigger can't fire a duplicate while this one runs. The claim word is 'Drafting' (NOT the reels' 'Building') because the carousel cloud job drafts the spec first; the GitHub worker then sets 'Ready to Post' on success or 'Render Failed' on error.

nodes.name: HTTP - Trigger Carousel Build
nodes.notes: Fire the build-carousel GitHub Action via repository_dispatch. client_payload.from_sheet='true' tells the workflow to AUTO-DRAFT the spec from the next 'Ready to Run' carousel row (draft_spec, cheap GPT text, usage-guarded) and then build it; to_drive='true' uploads the finished deck to Drive and emails the review link. The cloud worker does EVERYTHING else: draft spec -> per slide find real reference -> GPT-4o vision -> house-style prompt -> image -> render deck -> Drive -> review email -> write final Status + Drive URL back to this row. If GITHUB_REPO/GITHUB_PAT use $env and your server blocks $env, paste the literal repo ('owner/repo') and a 'Bearer <PAT>' token directly here instead.

parameters.subject: =Carousel build dispatch failed (row {{ $json.row_index }})
parameters.message: =The GitHub dispatch failed for carousel row {{ $json.row_index }} (topic: {{ $json.topic }}). Check Workflow C execution log. The row is left on 'Drafting' - reset it to 'Ready to Run' to retry.

nodes.name: Gmail - Notify Dispatch Failed
nodes.notes: Only fires if the GitHub dispatch HTTP call itself errors (bad PAT, repo typo, GitHub down). A normal build that ends in 'Render Failed' is handled by the cloud worker writing that Status back to the row, not by this email.

meta.notes: Workflow C - Carousel Builder (2026-06-19). The carousel sibling of Workflow B (tweet-card reels). Mirrors B exactly: on a 'Ready to Run' row it claims the row and fires a GitHub repository_dispatch; the cloud Action does all the real work. The ONLY differences from B: (1) it gates on 'Post Type' == 'carousel' so it ignores reel rows on the shared Reels tab; (2) the claim Status is 'Drafting' not 'Building'; (3) it dispatches event_type 'build-carousel' with client_payload.from_sheet='true' so the Action auto-drafts the spec before building. The matching reel workflow now also excludes 'Post Type' == 'carousel' so the two never fight over a row.
